summary: Acoustic gems, fiery second set and soaring third‑set jams
blurb: The first set delivers a tight acoustic groove highlighted by Iko Iko and Dark Hollow. The electric second set burns through Loser, Deal and a rapturous Little Red Rooster, while the third set strings together He's Gone → The Wheel → Stella Blue and an inspired Estimated Prophet. Overall the night is high‑energy with standout solos from Jerry and solid support throughout.
review:
On October 7 1980 the Dead rolled into the Warfield for a three‑set marathon that has become a touchstone of their early‑80s era. The acoustic opener – Iko Iko, Dark Hollow, Rosalie McFall and a plaintive El Paso segue into Ripple – set a warm, intimate tone that many fans still cite as a “high water mark.”  The second set flips the switch: Loser erupts into a tight groove, Deal rockets forward, and Jerry’s lead guitar goes “out of control,” especially on Little Red Rooster where his slide work is described as glue‑like.  Phil provides a steady bass backbone, punctuated by a memorable burst during Let It Grow.  Brent’s keyboards shine on Estimated Prophet, adding that signature ’80s texture while Mickey stays out of Space and returns with a light “Mickey Mouse” touch that still drives the rhythm.  The third set is a tour‑de‑force – He’s Gone → The Wheel → Stella Blue flows seamlessly into I Need A Miracle and Good Lovin’, capped by an Alabama Getaway encore.  Across all sources the show is praised for its energy, seamless segues, and Jerry’s soaring solos, though a few recordings note occasional mud in the third set and a brief digital hiccup on Don’t Ease Me In.

For listeners seeking the clearest picture of this night, the Owsley‑mixed soundboard capture (gd1980-10-07.108882.sbd‑stanley.blackwood‑waddell.flac16) offers excellent fidelity and a pristine mix that lets every nuance shine.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Two recordings are clearly positive, three are mixed and one is negative; the balance of commentary praises standout songs and Jerry’s solos while only a single source notes a performance flaw, yielding a moderate positive sentiment.

best_recording.reason: Excellent Owsley‑mixed soundboard capture with crystal‑clear fidelity and a clean, balanced mix that showcases the full band.

key_highlights: Acoustic first set delivers tight, warm grooves on Iko Iko and Dark Hollow
Jerry’s incendiary solos on Little Red Rooster and throughout the third set
Seamless segues He’s Gone → The Wheel → Stella Blue
Brent’s tasteful keyboards lift Estimated Prophet

band_performance.Jerry: Ripping solos dominate the night – a breakout first solo in set two, soaring leads on Little Red Rooster and an almost “out of control” energy that drives the third‑set jams.
band_performance.Phil: Provides a solid bass foundation with notable moments during Let It Grow; his playing underpins the band’s tightness throughout.
band_performance.Bob: His rhythm guitar presence feels intimate, especially in the acoustic set, giving listeners the sense of sitting right next to him onstage.
band_performance.Brent: Delivers tasteful 80s‑era keyboard textures, highlighted on Estimated Prophet and adding color to the electric numbers.
band_performance.Mickey: Stays out for most of Space, then adds a light “Mickey Mouse” touch on Alabama Getaway that still locks in the groove.
